document.docAuthor: Rojas-Buffet, Carolina, Bouzan, Rodrigo Salvador, Sierwald, Petra, Brescovit, Antonio Do- Mingos & Simó, Miguel
document.docTitle: Sandalodesmus joachimadisi Rojas-Buffet & Bouzan & Sierwald & Brescovit & Simó 2022, n. sp.
document.masterDocTitle: Sandalodesmus joachimadisi n. sp., the first Chelodesmidae member from Uruguay with an updated key to species of the genus (Diplopoda: Polydesmida)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Sandalodesmus joachimadisi n. sp., the first Chelodesmidae member from Uruguay with an updated key to species of the genus (Diplopoda: Polydesmida)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Sección Entomología, Facultad de Ciencias, Universidad de la República, Montevideo, Uruguay. & Departamento de Ecología y Biología Evolutiva, Instituto de Investigaciones Biológicas Clemente Estable, Ministerio de Educación y Cultura, Montevideo, Uruguay.

heading.emphasis:
Sandalodesmus joachimadisi Rojas-Buffet & Bouzan
, n. sp.

figureCitation.captionText-0: FIGURE 1. Sandalodesmus joachimadisi n. sp. from Valle del Lunarejo, Rivera, Uruguay. A. Live male. B. Two specimens found under cow dung, one of them inside a nest.
figureCitation.captionText-1: FIGURE 2. Somatic characters of Sandalodesmus joachimadisi n. sp. A. Holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), anterior body rings, dorsal view. B. Holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), middle body rings, dorsal view. C. Holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), posterior body rings, dorsal view. D. Non type ♂ (FCE-Myr 0556), left antenna. E. Non type ♂ (FCE-Myr 0556), ventral gnathochilarium. F. Non type ♂ (FCE-Myr 0556), dorsal gnathochilarium. G. Non type ♂ (FCE-Myr 0767), sternite 4 with a single pair of fused projections. H. Non type ♂ (FCE-Myr 0767), posterior sternites without modifications. Abbreviations: L: lingual lobes; M: mentum; O: ozopore; P: projection; S: stipes. Scale bars: 1.0 mm (A–D, G–H), 0.5 mm (E–F).
figureCitation.captionText-2: FIGURE 3. Sexual characters of Sandalodesmus joachimadisi n. sp. A. Holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), left gonopod, mesal view. B. Holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), left gonopod, lateral view. C. Holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), left gonopod, ventral view. D. Paratype ♀ (FCE-Myr 0664), left vulva, ventral view. Abbreviations: Ap: acropodital process; Ca: cannula; Cx: coxa; Ev: external valve; Iv: internal valve; Op: operculum; Pf: prefemur; PfP: prefemoral process; Pg: prostatic groove; S: solenomere; SpP: spiniform process. Scale bars: 0.5 mm (A–C), 0.2 mm (D). Photographed by: Damián Hagopián.
figureCitation.captionText-3: FIGURE 4. Gonopod drawings of Sandalodesmus joachimadisi n. sp., holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), left gonopod. A. Mesal view. B. Lateral view. C. Ventral view. Abbreviations: Ap: acropodital process; Ca: cannula; Cx: coxa; Pf: prefemur; PfP: prefemoral process; Pg: prostatic groove; S: solenomere; SpP: spiniform process. Scale bars: 0.5 mm.
figureCitation.captionText-4: FIGURE 5. Distribution map of Sandalodesmus species.

subSubSection.paragraph: Diagnosis. Males differ from all other species of the genus by the following combination of characters: prefemoral process (PfP) small, Z-shaped, twisted internally and directed parallel to acropodital branch ( Figs 3A , 4A ), prostatic groove (Pg) sinuous and S-shaped along the acropodite, ending upon an elongated prostatic apex ( Figs 3A , 4A ), acropodite process (Ap) broad and round, elongated, hood-shaped ( Figs 3A–C , 4A–C ), solenomere (S) apically curved, sickle-shaped ( Figs 3A , 4A ).

Name. The specific name is a patronymic in honor of the German ecologist and entomologist Joachim Ulrich Adis (1950–2007). He visited the Collection of Myriapoda of Facultad de Ciencias, Universidad de la República, Montevideo , Uruguay in 2005 and suggested the need for the study of the underexplored Uruguayan Diplopoda .

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 1.
Sandalodesmus joachimadisi
n. sp.
from Valle del Lunarejo, Rivera, Uruguay. A. Live male. B. Two specimens found under cow dung, one of them inside a nest.

paragraph.materialsCitation:
Holotype :
URUGUAY ● ♂ ; Valle del Lunarejo, Rivera , under cow dung, 31°11'23.7"S 55°54'04.2"W ;
27.XII.2018
; C. Rojas-Buffet leg.; FCE-Myr 0664.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Paratypes :
URUGUAY ● 3 ♀♀ ; same data as for holotype ; FCE-Myr 0664.

Additional material examined. All from URUGUAY ● 1 ♂ ; Tacuarembó Department , Tacuarembó city, urban zone, 31°44'18.9"S 55°58'46.7"W ; 14.XI.2013 ; A. Laborda leg.; FCE-Myr 0556 ● 2 ♀♀ ; same locality, walking at night, 31°44'19.63"S 55°58'46.60"W ; 27.XII.2018 ; D. Hagopián leg.; FCE-Myr 0665 ● 4 ♂♂ , 3 ♀♀ ; Rivera Department , Sierra de la Aurora, under stones, 31°03'18.8"S 55°42'58.5"W ; 10.I.1961 ; C. Carbonell and L.C. de Zolessi leg.; FCE-Myr 0354 ● 1 ♂ , 1 ♀ ; Valle del Lunarejo, riparian forest, 31°11'27''S 55°56'20''W ; 13.III.2019 ; C. Rojas-Buffet leg.; FCE-Myr 0768 ● 2 ♂♂ , 3 ♀♀ ; same locality, under cow dung, camping zone, 31°11'18.8''S 55°54'19.1''W ; 13.III.2019 ; C. Rojas-Buffet and M. Simó leg.; FCE-Myr 0769 ● 1 ♂ ; same locality, under cow dung, 31°10'21.5"S 55°54'49.4"W ; 16.XI.2019 ; E. Lorier leg.; FCE-Myr 0770 ● 2 ♂♂ ; same locality, two meters from the stream, 31°10'23.0"S 55°54'06.7"W ; 26.XI.2018 ; M.J. Albo leg.; FCE-Myr 0771 ● 13 ♂♂ , 1 ♀ ; Salto Department , Olivera, grassland, collected in pitfall trap, 31°05'22.0"S 56°46'57.0"W ; 10.XII.2019 ; G. Pompozzi leg.; FCE-Myr 0767.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 2 . Somatic characters of
Sandalodesmus joachimadisi
n. sp. A.
Holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), anterior body rings, dorsal view. B. Holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), middle body rings, dorsal view. C. Holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), posterior body rings, dorsal view. D . Non type ♂ (FCE-Myr 0556), left antenna. E. Non type ♂ (FCE-Myr 0556), ventral gnathochilarium. F. Non type ♂ (FCE-Myr 0556), dorsal gnathochilarium. G. Non type ♂ (FCE-Myr 0767), sternite 4 with a single pair of fused projections. H. Non type ♂ (FCE-Myr 0767), posterior sternites without modifications. Abbreviations: L : lingual lobes; M : mentum; O : ozopore; P : projection; S : stipes. Scale bars: 1.0 mm ( A–D , G–H ), 0.5 mm ( E–F ).

subSubSection.paragraph: Description. Male ( holotype , FCE-Myr 0664). Coloration in life: head reddish. Antennae reddish brown with distal end of antennomers yellowish, antennomere seven completely yellow ( Fig. 2D ). Body reddish, with paranota tip yellowish and metazonites whitish at posterior border ( Figs 1 , 2A–C ). Gnathochilarium ( Fig. 2E, F ): mentum ( M) with smooth edges and small thick setae at central region; apical edge of stipes ( S) and lingual lobes ( L) with long and thin setae. Body rings: cuticle smooth; paranota with standard polydesmidan pore formula (5, 7, 9, 10, 12, 13, 15–19); paranota sub-triangular ( Fig. 2A–C ); ozopores ( O ) situated posteriorly ( Fig. 2B, C ). Stigma oval. Sternite 4 with a single pair of fused projections ( P) ( Fig. 2G ). Other sternites without modifications ( Fig. 2H ). Anterior sternites covered with short and thick setae, posterior ones sparsely covered with thin setae. Leg pair 2 coxae each with a rectangular-shaped genital papilla. Walking legs reddish white-brown and without modifications ( Fig. 1 ). Gonopod aperture on seventh body ring oval, posterior edge straight and without folds, but slightly elevated in anal view. Telson anteriorly reddish, posterior border yellowish ( Fig. 2C ).

Gonopods ( Figs 3 , 4 ): coxae ( Cx ) almost same length as telopodite, prominent in lateral view ( Figs 3A–C , 4 ), with two anterior macrosetae ( Figs 3B , 4B ) and with a single, large and slender spiniform process ( SpP ) (Figs Total length ( holotype , FCE-Myr 0664) (fragmented specimen): 39.8. Width: 4.47. Antennomere lengths (1 to 7): 0.42; 0.94; 0.93; 0.95; 0.97; 1.00; 0.24. Podomeres lengths of leg 10 (1 to 7): 0.49; 0.80; 1.53; 0.53; 0.63; 1.00; 0.30. Gonopod aperture: 1.0 long, 1.9 wide. Telson: 1.01 long. Gonopod: 1.69 long, 1.83 wide. Gonocoxae: 0.83 long, 1.09 wide. Telopodite: 1.57 long, 0.72 wide.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 4 . Gonopod drawings of
Sandalodesmus joachimadisi
n. sp.
, holotype ♂ (FCE-Myr 0664), left gonopod. A. Mesal view. B. Lateral view. C. Ventral view. Abbreviations: Ap: acropodital process; Ca: cannula; Cx: coxa; Pf: prefemur; PfP: prefemoral process; Pg: prostatic groove; S: solenomere; SpP: spiniform process. Scale bars : 0.5 mm.

FIGURE 5. Distribution map of
Sandalodesmus species.

subSubSection.paragraph: Natural history. The species is usually associated with urban environments, such as built-up areas or camping areas. It can also be found in natural areas, such as riparian forest or grasslands. Specimens were found under stones, under decaying tree trunks, under cow dung, or walking on grass. Some specimens were found together in nests ( Fig. 1B ) that may have been built by the millipedes. Frequently more than one individual was found at the collection sites.
